jobs.title: Environmental Characterization/Remediation Geologist/Hydrogeologist
jobs.description:
At Jacobs, we're challenging today to reinvent tomorrow by solving the world's most critical problems for thriving cities, resilient environments, mission-critical outcomes, operational advancement, scientific discovery and cutting-edge manufacturing, turning abstract ideas into realities that transform the world for good.

Your impact

We’re seeking an environmental geologist/hydrogeologist with a background in environmental site characterization and remediation to support the delivery of challenging projects for government and global private sector clients.

Based out of the California area, you’ll work with other scientists, engineers, subject matter experts, and project managers on exciting projects in which you will learn from others and pass along your knowledge. Responsibilities may include designing, performing, or overseeing soil, soil vapor, groundwater, and surface water investigations, working among teams of colleagues and subcontractors, coordinating and managing fieldwork logistics, data analysis and interpretation, and technical report writing. You'll work alongside recognized technical experts, developing and implementing comprehensive site strategies, and delivering innovative solutions for some of our most challenging site issues.

Projects require thoughtful strategy development, leveraging technical expertise, and collaboration with clients and stakeholders. All projects must be delivered with the high safety, compliance, and quality standards expected by both Jacobs and our client. Our global team is here to provide you with the tools and support needed to grow your skills and career, as well as those of your colleagues. By driving consistency and innovation across projects, you'll help your team achieve client goals. Bring your positive attitude and strong, collaborative style, and together, we'll tackle complex environmental remediation challenges and solve our client’s problems.

At Jacobs, we’re partnering across the globe to create the best project outcomes by maximizing the design, digital technology, and support capabilities of our Global Integrated Delivery (GID) teammates. By joining Jacobs, you’ll commit to supporting and engaging with these teams, as we work to build a company like no other.

Here's what you'll need

Bachelor's degree in geology or other closely related field.
At least 3 years of experience delivering environmental site characterization and remediation projects, including fieldwork methodology/procedures and assessment/evaluation of data for various environmental media (e.g., soil, sediments, groundwater)
Experience conducting environmental remediation site investigation field work, working on project sites, and overseeing subcontractors.
Experience working with multi-disciplinary teams toward a common goal.
40-hr HAZWOPER certification or the ability to attain this certification
Effective verbal and written communication skills with coworkers and contractors.
Strong attention to detail when conducting field work and evaluating data.

Our health and welfare benefits are designed to invest in you, and in the things you care about. Your health. Your well-being. Your security. Your future. Employees have access to medical, dental, vision, and basic life insurance, a 401(k) plan, and the ability to purchase company stock at a discount. Eligible employees may also enroll in a deferred compensation plan or the Executive Deferral Plan. Jacobs has an unlimited U.S. Personalized Paid Time Off (PPTO) policy for full-time salaried/exempt employees, seven paid holidays, and caregiver leave. And certain roles may be eligible for additional rewards, including merit increases, performance discretionary bonus, and stock.

The base salary range for this position is $70,900.00 to $107,250.00. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or protected veteran status. Job posted on July 02, 2026. This position will be open for at least 3 days.

We value collaboration and believe that in-person interactions are crucial for both our culture and client delivery. We empower employees with our hybrid working policy, allowing them to split their work week between Jacobs offices/projects and remote locations enabling them to deliver their best work.

Your application experience is important to us, and we’re keen to adapt to make every interaction even better. If you require further support or reasonable adjustments with regards to the recruitment process (for example, you require the application form in a different format), please contact the team via Careers Support.

Show less

Seniority level

Not Applicable

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Research, Analyst, and Information Technology

Industries

Business Consulting and Services

jobs.company: North West Queensland Regional Organisation of Councils
jobs.title: Regional Waste Behaviour Change and Environmental Health Coordinator
jobs.description:
Company Description

The North West Queensland Regional Organisation of Councils (NWQROC) represents 12 councils within the North West Queensland region, including Burke, Carpentaria, Cloncurry, Croydon, Doomadgee, Etheridge, Flinders, Kowanyama, McKinlay, Mornington Island, Mount Isa, and Richmond. The organisation supports regional collaboration by addressing shared challenges and opportunities. It works to enhance regional development, advocate for community needs, and foster resilience across the region. The organisation aims to improve outcomes for the communities it represents through innovative and strategic initiatives .

Role Description

The Regional Waste Behaviour Change and Environmental Health Coordinator is a full-time hybrid role based in North West Queensland, with the option to work from home for part of the week. This role c oordinates the planning and delivery of Environmental Health officer functions across NWQROC members, including food license inspections and investigations into environmental nuisances under relevant legislation, as well as coordinating the delivery of regional programs that encourage waste reduction, recycling, and sustainable resource use across NWQROC member councils.

Qualifications

Tertiary qualifications in Applied Scene (Environmental Health) or other Environmental Health Australia (EHA) accredited course from a recognised tertiary institution, which is relevant to the tasks and responsibilities of the position.
Demonstrated ability to interpret and apply legislation with an understanding of the context in which Environmental Health Services are delivered at a local government level.
An ability to conduct compliance inspections and follow through with the appropriate enforcement actions.
Demonstrated organisational, time management and prioritisation skills with a proven ability to balance competing workloads and deliver within deadlines. 
Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written. 
Excellent standard of IT literacy and advanced computer and software skills. 
Ability to work independently, flexibly and remotely – as needed.
Highspeed and reliable internet connection to host Teams meetings. 
Minimum of Queensland C class provisional drivers’ licence. 

Remuneration

Contracted position to 30 June 2028.
Remuneration - $115,000/annum plus Super.

Closing date

The NWQROC will be shortlisting and interviewing applicants as they are lodged.

Further information

For a copy of the position description or a confidential discussion regarding this role, please do not hesitate to contact Josh Dyke at josh.dyke@nwqroc.com.au or 0473 795 066.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Mid-Senior level

Employment type

Full-time

Industries

Government Administration
